name: TQQQ Intrabar Vol Breakout
description: Uses a 240-bar average intrabar volatility (|open−close|/open) on TQQQ minute bars to identify low-vol regimes, entering 100% when vol < 0.1 and price is within 2% of the 240-minute high. Exits on vol spike > 0.15 or a 3% hard stop from entry.
overfit_notes: Three thresholds: vol entry 0.1, vol exit 0.15, proximity to high 98%, 3% stop. The vol entry/exit pair is calibrated to a specific breakout pattern and is somewhat arbitrary; the proximity filter is reasonable.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Avg intrabar vol (240-bar) < 0.1 AND price ≥ 240-min high × 0.98: 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Resolution
rules.text: Minute bars; active only after 10:00 AM ET

name: Tech Dip Buy
description: Buys the top 5 US technology stocks by market cap when they pull back hard during an uptrend, then holds until they recover to new highs or the loss gets too large. Universe rotates automatically as market caps shift. Equal-weight across held positions up to 20% per slot.
overfit_notes: Textbook RSI(2)/SMA(50) entry with a clean 15% hard stop; dynamic universe removes hindsight symbol selection. Only mild concern is the small trade count (~80 trades over 11 years).

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 5 US tech stocks by market cap (Morningstar Technology sector)

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: RSI(2) < 30 AND price > SMA(50): up to 20% per name

name: TQQQ 60% Annual Rebalance
description: Holds a static 60% allocation to TQQQ, rebalancing once per year on the first trading day of each year to correct drift. Serves as a leveraged baseline — no signal logic, just a constant leveraged Nasdaq position.
overfit_notes: Single parameter: 60% allocation. The round number is a deliberate design choice, not a swept value. Minimal overfit risk.

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Annual — first trading day of each year, resets to 60%

rules.label: No signal
rules.text: No entry/exit logic; pure static allocation

name: QQQ RSI(2) Dip → Equal-Weight TQQQ/SOXL/TECL
description: Enters an equal-weight basket of TQQQ, SOXL, and TECL (3× leveraged tech) when QQQ's 2-period Wilder RSI drops below 20, capturing short-term oversold bounces in leveraged Nasdaq. Exits fully when RSI recovers above 20.
overfit_notes: Two parameters: RSI period 2 and threshold 20. Both are canonical for ultra-short mean reversion. The three-name basket is a deliberate diversification, not a swept choice.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: QQQ RSI(2, Wilder) < 20: 33% TQQQ + 33% SOXL + 33% TECL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / SOXL / TECL

name: TQQQ Dynamic Sizing: SMA200 + RSI Tiers
description: Three-tier TQQQ sizing gated by the 200-day SMA. Above SMA: 100% on RSI(2) < 30 dip, 50% default, 20% on RSI(10) overbought > 80. Below SMA: cash. Adapts position size to both trend regime and short-term momentum state.
overfit_notes: Five parameters: SMA 200, RSI2 period/threshold, RSI10 period/threshold, three allocation levels (100/50/20%). The allocation tiers feel calibrated; the combination of two RSI indicators on the same asset adds fitting risk.

rules.label: Default
rules.text: 50% TQQQ when in regime with no RSI signal

name: TQQQ Expanding Range Breakout + ATR Trailing Stop
description: Enters TQQQ when QQQ is in an uptrend (above SMA 200), the previous day's bar range expanded vs. the bar before it, and ADX(10) > 25 confirming trend strength. Manages the trade with a 3×ATR(14) Wilder trailing stop and exits at the 20-day high or on QQQ falling below SMA.
overfit_notes: Five parameters: SMA 200, ADX 10/threshold 25, ATR 14/multiplier 3, 20-day exit high. The combination of range expansion + ADX is somewhat fitted; the ATR multiplier 3 is standard.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: QQQ > SMA(200) AND prior bar range > bar-before range AND ADX(10) > 25: 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Trailing stop
rules.text: Price − 3 × ATR(14, Wilder); ratchets up, never down

name: QQQ SMA(150) Trend → TQQQ
description: Holds 100% TQQQ when QQQ is above its 150-day SMA, otherwise moves to cash. A slight variant of the standard 200-day trend rule — uses 150 days for a faster regime signal on the underlying index.
overfit_notes: Single parameter: SMA 150. Slightly shorter than the canonical 200 but still a widely-used period. The period choice could be a minor fit to history.

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open (only on regime change)

name: TQQQ IBS Extreme + ATR Stop
description: Buys TQQQ when the Internal Bar Strength (IBS = (close−low)/(high−low)) is below 0.1, signaling a close near the day's low (capitulation). Exits when IBS exceeds 0.9 (close near day's high) or price falls below entry minus 3×ATR(14).
overfit_notes: Three parameters: IBS entry 0.1, IBS exit 0.9, ATR multiplier 3. IBS thresholds 0.1/0.9 are at the extreme ends of [0,1] and are standard for this type of reversal strategy. ATR multiplier 3 is conventional.

rules.label: Stop loss
rules.text: Close < entry price − 3 × ATR(14, Wilder): liquidate

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 45 min after market open (previous day's completed bar)

name: Top-5 Market Cap + IBS Bear Dip / Bull Equal-Weight
description: Dynamically selects the top 5 US stocks by market cap. In a bull regime (QQQ above SMA 200), holds them equal-weighted. In a bear regime, applies an IBS filter — only holds names closing near their daily low (IBS < 0.2), equal-weighted among those that qualify.
overfit_notes: Three parameters: top-5 universe, SMA 200, IBS bear threshold 0.2. The IBS threshold (0.2 vs the more extreme 0.1) in bear mode is the main fitting risk; 0.2 allows more positions than a pure extremes-only filter.

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 5 US stocks by market cap (HasFundamentalData, Price > 5)

rules.label: Bear (QQQ ≤ SMA200)
rules.text: Equal weight names with IBS < 0.2; cash if none qualify
